user_query:
Please read the two research paper excerpts under input/ and produce strict 250-word structured abstracts for each. Deliverables:

1) Two markdown abstracts:
- output/abstracts/paper1.md
- output/abstracts/paper2.md
Each abstract must have exactly five sections with headers and colons: Background, Objective, Methods, Results, Conclusion. Keep to exactly 250 words per abstract (not including headers). Preserve all key quantitative findings (sample sizes, effect sizes, p-values, confidence intervals, percentages) and ensure every number in each abstract exactly matches its source file—do not invent numbers.

2) A batch index CSV at output/abstracts/index.csv with columns: paper_id,word_count,has_all_sections,numeric_count. Include one row for paper1 and one for paper2. word_count must be 250 for both; has_all_sections should reflect whether all five sections are present; numeric_count should be the count of numeric items you included in the abstract.

3) A verification JSON at output/abstracts/verification.json mapping each paper_id to the list of numeric strings you used (e.g., sample sizes, percentages, p-values, CIs) so they can be cross-checked.

4) A short comparative summary at output/abstracts/summary.md with 3–5 bullet points contrasting the two studies (discipline, methods, principal quantitative outcomes, limitations implied by the text). Keep it concise and factual.

Inputs to read:
- input/paper1.txt (biomedical RCT excerpt with sample sizes, endpoints, p-values, and CIs)
- input/paper2.txt (computer systems/algorithms paper excerpt with benchmarks, speedups, tail latency, CIs, and one p-value)

Important: Maintain numerical fidelity to the sources. Include p-values and confidence intervals in the abstracts if present in the source. Adapt style appropriately to each discipline while keeping the five-section structure.
